resource.description: Which builder playbook to fetch. Accepts either a bare id (one of: authenticating-as-the-user, authoring-new-agents, choosing-the-model, cost-and-quota-analysis, debugging-sessions, designing-mcp-surfaces, editing-agents-safely, platform-mental-model, querying-ai-observability, reading-an-agent, running-and-evaluating-tests, secrets-and-integrations, setting-up-slack-app) or its URI form (`posthog://agent-platform/playbooks/<id>`). A playbook is a markdown guide for using the agent-platform authoring tools well; it comes back with the live, scope-aware tool surface for the operation. Fetch the playbook rather than recalling tool names from memory.

name.description: Short human label for the source. Shown in the settings list and in agent citations.

url.description: Public HTTP(S) URL to fetch. Private or internal hosts are rejected.

source_type.description: Source type. Always "url" for this tool.

refresh_interval.description: How often to auto-refresh this source in the background. Defaults to "manual" (no auto-refresh).

always_include.description: When true, this source's content is injected into every support reply prompt as general context (tone, policies, direction), not just when it matches a query.

message.description: Optional message to include in the debug data

id.description: The email template id to edit.
Id of the content block to update.

patch.description:
Partial content-block fields, deep-merged into the existing block; a null leaf deletes that key. e.g. {values: {text: '

Hi

'}} changes only the block's text.

id.description: Id of the column to update.

patch.description: Partial column fields, deep-merged; a null leaf deletes that key.

id.description: Id of the row to update.

patch.description: Partial row fields, deep-merged; a null leaf deletes that key.

column_id.description: Id of the column to insert the block into.

content.description: A content block {type, values: {...}}; omit id and values._meta — they are assigned server-side. type is one of text, heading, button, image, divider, html, etc.

index.description: 0-based insert position; omit to append to the end.

id.description: Id of the content block to remove.
Id of the content block to move.

index.description: 0-based position in the destination column; omit to append.

row.description: A full row {cells, columns: [{contents: [...], values}], values}; ids and Unlayer numbering are assigned server-side for the row and everything nested in it.

index.description: 0-based insert position; omit to append to the end.

id.description: Id of the row to remove.

operations.description: Ordered edits applied atomically to the template's Unlayer design: the stored design is read, the ops are applied in order, the result is validated and re-rendered to HTML, and saved only if valid — otherwise the template is left unchanged. Reference blocks by id so you never resend the whole design.

description.description: Description explaining when the event is triggered

tags.description: Tags to organize events by product area (e.g. "checkout", "onboarding") or user journey stage (e.g. "acquisition", "activation", "monetization", "retention")

verified.description: Mark as verified to indicate the event is properly instrumented and tracking correctly

hidden.description: Mark event as no longer used/captured. Hides it from UI while preserving historical data

eventName.description: The name of the event to update (e.g. "$pageview", "user_signed_up")

description.description: Description explaining when the event is triggered

tags.description: Tags to organize events by product area (e.g. "checkout", "onboarding") or user journey stage (e.g. "acquisition", "activation", "monetization", "retention")

verified.description: Mark as verified to indicate the event is properly instrumented and tracking correctly

hidden.description: Mark event as no longer used/captured. Hides it from UI while preserving historical data

data.description: The event definition data to update

query.description: The final SQL query to be executed.

truncate.description: Whether to truncate large blob/JSON values in results. Defaults to true. Set to false when you need full untruncated results (e.g., for dumping to a file).

connectionId.description: Optional id of an external data source (e.g. a Postgres, DuckDB, or MySQL direct-query connection). When set, runs the query against that source instead of the ClickHouse catalog. Use external-data-sources-list to discover available connection ids.

id.description: The ID of the experiment to get comprehensive results for

refresh.description: Force refresh of results instead of using cached values. Defaults to false.

ExternalDataJobsAfterSchema.description: ISO timestamp — only return jobs created after this date (e.g. "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z").

ExternalDataJobsBeforeSchema.description: ISO timestamp — only return jobs created before this date (e.g. "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z").

ExternalDataJobsSchemasSchema.description: Filter jobs by table schema names (e.g. ["users", "orders"]). Only returns jobs for these tables.

ExternalDataSourcePayloadSchema.description: Connection credentials for the source. Keys depend on source_type. For database sources: host, port, database, user, password, schema. For SaaS sources: api_key or OAuth fields. For source_type "Custom" (a user-defined REST API): `manifest_json` (a stringified RESTAPIConfig describing client.base_url, auth, and resources) plus the credential for the auth type declared in the manifest — `auth_token` (bearer), `auth_api_key` (api_key), or `auth_password` (http_basic); keep secrets in these auth_* keys, never inline in manifest_json. Use external-data-sources-wizard (pass source_type) to see required fields per source type. For the advanced external-data-sources-create flow, the per-table 'schemas' array (built from external-data-sources-db-schema) also goes in here, e.g. {"host": ..., "password": ..., "schemas": [{"name": "orders", "should_sync": true, "sync_type": "incremental", "incremental_field": "updated_at", "incremental_field_type": "datetime"}]}. Do not pass unresolved {"secretRef": ...} objects — resolve secrets to real values first, or use a credential_id from data-warehouse-source-connect-link.

ExternalDataSourceTypeSchema.description: The source type name (e.g. "Postgres", "MySQL", "Stripe"). Use external-data-sources-wizard to see available types and their required fields.

summary.description: A one-sentence headline capturing the feedback (e.g. "session replay scrubber jumps backwards when you click the timeline", "query-trends descriptions made it hard to choose between trends and funnels", or "the new SQL editor autocomplete is excellent").

feedback_type.description: What this feedback is about. "product" = any PostHog product or feature (insights, session replay, feature flags, the data warehouse, web analytics, error tracking, etc.). "mcp" = this MCP server itself — a tool, its input schema, response format, an error, or these instructions. "docs" = PostHog documentation. "other" = anything that doesn't fit the above.

sentiment.description: The overall tone. Use "negative" for something broken or blocking, "mixed" for mostly-fine-but-with-a-concrete-problem, "neutral" for a suggestion or feature request with no strong sentiment, and "positive" for praise or something that worked well. All sentiments are welcome — positive feedback is encouraged, not just problems.

product_area.description: The PostHog product or area this is about, in free text (e.g. "session replay", "insights", "data warehouse", "feature flags", "docs"). Most useful for product feedback; for MCP feedback the tool name belongs in `details`/`friction_points` instead.

category.description: For MCP feedback (`feedback_type: "mcp"`) only: the single category that best describes the dominant theme. Pick "missing_tool" if a capability was absent, "tool_description" if the tool docs were unclear, "tool_input_schema" if input args were confusing, "tool_output_format" if the response was hard to consume, "instructions_clarity" if these MCP instructions were unclear, "tool_correctness" if a tool returned wrong data, "error_message" if an error was unhelpful, "performance" if latency was the issue. Omit for product, docs, or other feedback.

task_completed.description: Were you able to complete the user's task? Be honest — "false" is just as useful as "true". Most relevant when `feedback_type` is "mcp".

tools_used.description: The MCP tool names you called while working on the user's task (e.g. ["read-data-schema", "query-trends"]). Helps us correlate feedback to specific tools.

friction_points.description: Clear, concise bullet points describing the friction — what was confusing, broken, slow, or missing. Quote the exact product surface, tool name, parameter, or error text where you can. Omit for purely positive feedback.

suggested_improvement.description: The single most impactful, concrete change that would address this feedback, if you can name one (e.g. "add a `filters` example to query-funnel's description", or "let the replay scrubber snap to the nearest event"). Optional — praise or an observation doesn't need one.

user_request.description: A short, anonymised paraphrase of what the user originally asked you to do. Do not include PII, customer names, or sensitive query content.

details.description: Any additional context that doesn't fit the other fields. Keep it to clear, concise bullet points.

insightId.description: The insight ID or short_id to run.

output_format.description: Output format. "optimized" returns a human-readable summary from server-side formatters (recommended for analysis). "json" returns the raw query results as JSON.

variables_override.description: Object (or pre-encoded JSON string) to override the insight's HogQL variables for this run only (not persisted). Format: {"<variable_id>": {"code_name": "<code_name>", "variableId": "<variable_id>", "value": <new_value>}}. Each entry must include `code_name` — partial entries are silently dropped. The simplest workflow is to call `insight-get` first, copy the matching entry from the response's query variables, and mutate `value`. Top-level keys replace; nested values are not deep-merged. Ignored when accessed via a sharing token.

filters_override.description: Object (or pre-encoded JSON string) to override the insight's filters for this run only (not persisted). Top-level keys replace; nested values are not deep-merged — pass the complete value for any key you override. Accepts the same keys as the dashboard filters schema (e.g., `date_from`, `date_to`, `properties`). Ignored when accessed via a sharing token.

search.description: Optional substring filter applied to prompt names and prompt content.

content.description: Controls how much prompt content is included in list results. 'full' includes the full prompt, 'preview' includes a short prompt_preview, and 'none' omits prompt content entirely.

limit.description: Number of events to return per page.

offset.description: Number of events to skip for pagination.

event_name.description: The name of the event that you want to retrieve properties for.

entity.description: The type of the entity that you want to retrieve properties for.

action_id.description: The ID of the action that you want to retrieve properties for.

entity.description: The type of the entity that you want to retrieve properties for.

property_name.description: Verified property name of an entity.
Verified property name of an event.
Verified property name of an action.

query.description: The data schema query to execute.

id.description: ID of an existing shared/saved metric. Discover IDs with experiment-saved-metrics-list.

metadata.description: Optional per-link metadata. Omit to default this metric to primary.

type.description: Whether this metric is a primary or secondary metric on the experiment.

SavedMetricsAttachSchema.description: The complete desired set of shared (saved) metrics for the experiment — this REPLACES all existing saved-metric links, it does not append. To add or remove one, first read the experiment's current saved_metrics via experiment-get and resend the full set. Pass an empty array to detach all shared metrics.

key.description: Stable option key — lowercase letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens. Sent back when this option is selected.

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the review UI.

options.description: Ordered categorical options. Must contain at least one option with unique keys.

selection_mode.description: Whether reviewers select one option or many. Defaults to "single".

min_selections.description: Minimum selections required. Only valid when selection_mode is "multiple".

max_selections.description: Maximum selections allowed. Only valid when selection_mode is "multiple".

anyOf.description: Config shape used when kind is "categorical".

max.description: Optional inclusive maximum score (must be ≥ min).

step.description: Optional increment step for numeric input, e.g. 1 or 0.5.

anyOf.description: Config shape used when kind is "numeric".

true_label.description: Optional label shown for the true branch (e.g. "Yes").

false_label.description: Optional label shown for the false branch (e.g. "No").

anyOf.description: Config shape used when kind is "boolean".

ScoreDefinitionConfigSchema.description: Immutable scorer configuration. Pick the shape matching the scorer kind: categorical (options + selection_mode), numeric (min/max/step), or boolean (true_label/false_label). The server validates the shape against the kind on the parent scorer and returns 400 on a mismatch.

table_name.description: Name of a synced data warehouse table. Use `external-data-schemas-list` to discover available tables.

timestamp_field.description: Timestamp column or HogQL expression on the row (e.g. "created" or "toDateTime(created_at)"). Use `execute-sql` (`SELECT * FROM <table> LIMIT 1`) to inspect available columns.

key_field.description: Column on the row whose value matches the entity key. v1 supports group profiles only — the column value is compared to the group_key.

events.description: Events to count or sum over.

UsageMetricFiltersSchema.description: Filter definition. Pick exactly one branch: `data_warehouse` (set `source: "data_warehouse"` plus `table_name`/`timestamp_field`/`key_field`) or `events` (HogFunction filter shape with an `events` array).

id.description: The workflow (HogFlow) id to edit. Draft only — active workflows are read-only via MCP.
Id of the action to update.

patch.description: Partial action fields, deep-merged into the existing action; a null leaf deletes that key. e.g. {config: {inputs: {subject: {value: "Hi"}}}} changes only that one input.

action.description: A full action node {id, name, type, config, ...}; same shape as entries in the workflow actions array.

id.description: Id of the action to remove. Its incoming edges reconnect to its first outgoer.

type.description: 'continue' = fall-through (sequential or no-match path); 'branch' = a condition/cohort branch (needs index).

index.description: Required for type=branch: which condition/cohort slot this branch matches (0-based).

type.description: 'continue' = fall-through (sequential or no-match path); 'branch' = a condition/cohort branch (needs index).

index.description: Required for type=branch: which condition/cohort slot this branch matches (0-based).

edge.description: The edge to remove (matched on from/to/type/index).

id.description: Action id whose outgoing edges are being replaced.

type.description: 'continue' = fall-through (sequential or no-match path); 'branch' = a condition/cohort branch (needs index).

index.description: Required for type=branch: which condition/cohort slot this branch matches (0-based).

edges.description: The complete set of the action's outgoing edges; incoming edges are preserved.

operations.description: Ordered graph edits applied atomically: the stored graph is read, ops are applied in order, the result is fully validated, and it is saved only if valid — otherwise the workflow is left unchanged. Reference nodes/edges by id so you never resend the whole graph. The full updated workflow is returned.
